assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Parsed CVE Data table correctly lists: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = '< 0.11.14 (versions before 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the Ecosystem Detection section states: 'Library: quinn-proto (Rust crate)', 'Ecosystem: Cargo', 'Lock file: Cargo.lock', 'Check command: git show <tag>:Cargo.lock | grep -A2 "name = "quinn-proto""'.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: The security-matrix-mock.md lists 7 versions across two streams: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (2.1.x stream) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (2.2.x stream). The version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0 (v0.3.8), 2.1.1 (v0.3.12), 2.2.0 (v0.4.5), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12). No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, each version row uses the specific pinned Build Tag from the supportability matrix: 2.1.0 uses v0.3.8, 2.1.1 uses v0.3.12, 2.2.0 uses v0.4.5, 2.2.1 uses v0.4.8, 2.2.2 uses v0.4.9, 2.2.3 uses v0.4.11, 2.2.4 uses v0.4.12. These match the pinned tags in security-matrix-mock.md's supportability matrix, not branch HEAD.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, version 2.2.2 is identified as a retag with notes: 'retag of 2.2.1 (same as v0.4.8, quinn-proto 0.11.12)'. The quinn-proto column shows '--' (indicating no independent lookup) and the Affected? column shows 'YES', carrying forward the affected status from 2.2.1. This matches the security-matrix-mock.md which marks 2.2.2 as 'backend retag of 2.2.1' with tag v0.4.8.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the 'Current vs Proposed Affects Versions' table shows: Current (PSIRT-assigned) = 'RHTPA 2.0.0', Proposed (lock file evidence) = 'RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2'. The Comment to Post section shows the diff: 'Corrected Affects Versions: [RHTPA 2.0.0] -> [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. The Rationale section explains why RHTPA 2.0.0 is incorrect and lists lock-file evidence for each version.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the proposed versions are referenced by name: 'RHTPA 2.2.0', 'RHTPA 2.2.1', 'RHTPA 2.2.2'. In the Proposed Jira Mutation section, the version IDs use placeholder format '<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>', '<RHTPA-2.2.1-jira-id>', '<RHTPA-2.2.2-jira-id>' rather than hardcoded numeric Jira version IDs. The output references version names from the supportability matrix throughout.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md: Task 1 includes Repository (backend), Target Branch (release/0.4.z), Acceptance Criteria (3 checkboxes), and Dependencies (TC-8001). Task 2 includes Repository (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), Target Branch (main), Acceptance Criteria (2 checkboxes), and Dependencies (upstream backport task, TC-8001). The Linkage Summary confirms: 'Task 2 (downstream) linked to Task 1 (upstream) with "Blocks"'. Same pattern for Tasks 3 and 4 (2.1.x stream).

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, the Ecosystem section states 'Cargo (Source Dependency) — Two tasks per affected stream: upstream backport + downstream propagation.' For the 2.2.x stream: Task 1 is 'Upstream Backport (2.2.x stream)' and Task 2 is 'Downstream Propagation (2.2.x stream)'. The Linkage Summary confirms: 'Task 2 (downstream) linked to Task 1 (upstream) with "Blocks"'. Same two-task pattern is used for the 2.1.x stream (Tasks 3 and 4) with 'Task 4 (downstream) linked to Task 3 (upstream) with "Blocks"'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the section is titled 'Proposed Jira Mutation' (not 'Executed Mutation'), and the table header says 'Proposed (lock file evidence)'. The mutation code is presented as a proposal showing what would be done, not as confirmation of execution. In remediation.md, the Triage Outcome section uses 'Create remediation tasks' language (describing what should be done). The 'Comment to Post' section frames changes descriptively. Throughout all output files, changes are described as proposals/recommendations rather than confirmed executed actions.
